channel.tagline: True stories of courage, repentance, martyrdom, and holiness.
channel.description: Cinematic stories of Christian saints from the Orthodox and Catholic traditions: desert fathers, martyrs, missionaries, mystics, confessors, and holy fools.
channel.tone: reverent, cinematic, historically careful, emotionally strong

source_policy.primary_rule: Use public-domain books for story material and official church sources for verification. Do not copy modern copyrighted biographies verbatim.
source_policy.legend_rule: Label legendary or tradition-based material clearly instead of presenting every account as modern historical certainty.
source_policy.sensitivity_rule: Handle martyrdom, relics, icons, miracles, and denominational differences respectfully.

first_backlog.title: The Desert Saint Who Fought Demons Alone
first_backlog.angle: A wealthy young man abandons everything, enters the desert, and becomes the father of monasticism.

first_backlog.title: The Woman Who Vanished Into the Desert
first_backlog.angle: A life of radical repentance after a shocking conversion at the doors of the church.

first_backlog.title: The Real Saint Nicholas Was More Dangerous Than Santa
first_backlog.angle: The bishop behind the legend: charity, courage, imprisonment, and defense of the vulnerable.

first_backlog.title: The Bishop Who Walked Into Fire
first_backlog.angle: An elderly disciple of the apostles refuses to deny Christ before execution.

first_backlog.title: The Outlaw Who Became a Desert Father
first_backlog.angle: A violent robber becomes a monk, spiritual father, and martyr.
